name: MCAT content outline coverage map (content-category grain)
source_note: Reflects the structure of the AAMC MCAT content outline: the 10 Foundational Concepts and their 31 discipline 'content categories' (ids 1A..10A). Category names here are short paraphrases written for this tool, NOT the AAMC's copyrighted outline text. Only the id/name/weight structure is reproduced. The Critical Analysis and Reasoning Skills (CARS) section is skills-based and has no content categories, so it contributes no topics (see cars_note).
cars_note: CARS has no content categories in the AAMC outline; it is assessed as a reasoning skill over unseen passages, so it is intentionally absent from this content-coverage map.
weighting: Each topic 'weight' is a documented emphasis ESTIMATE, not an official AAMC number. Natural-science foundational concepts (biochemistry/biology/chemistry/physics) get higher weights because that material is tested across the two science sections and carries a heavier discrete/quantitative load; psychology/sociology concepts get lower weights. Rationale is per Foundational Concept (all content categories in a concept inherit its weight). Set every weight to 1.0 to disable weighting (weighted coverage then equals unweighted coverage).
min_coverage_note: Mirrors rslib MIN_COVERAGE (rslib/src/speedrun/readiness.rs). Readiness abstains (sufficient=false) while unweighted coverage < 0.50. Weighted coverage is reported as a sharper signal for skipped high-weight sections.

sections.Bio/Biochem: Biological and Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems
sections.Chem/Phys: Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems
sections.Psych/Soc: Psychological, Social, and Biological Foundations of Behavior
